accepted_rule: Use only sec_d person rows whose case_id+memid appears in ihs2_individ.dta.
remaining_caveat: Six nonroster health rows and two roster-only rows are excluded from person-level access outcomes; raw IDs are not exported.

accepted_rule: Exclude nonroster health rows from person-level access outcomes and report them as documented exclusions.
remaining_caveat: This does not prove full roster-module reconciliation for every possible health construct.

accepted_rule: Do not impute health need or access outcomes for roster persons absent from sec_d.
evidence: Roster-only rows have no health-module observation and are kept out of the access denominator.
remaining_caveat: This is a no-imputation rule, not evidence of no illness or no forgone care.

accepted_rule: Define acute health need as d04==1 among roster-matched sec_d rows.
remaining_caveat: Chronic need and hospitalization are context/mechanism only unless separately promoted.

accepted_rule: Among roster-matched d04==1 rows, count a person once if d07a==2 or d07b==2.
evidence: d07a/d07b code 2 label=Did nothing, no money; matched_no_money=631; all_health_no_money=631; construction_summary_no_money=631.
remaining_caveat: This verifies cost-barrier forgone care, not distance/supply access barriers.

accepted_rule: Do not repair d07a/d07b values outside d04==1; exclude them from the acute-need denominator and report the leakage.
evidence: d07a_leakage=6; d07b_leakage=0; d07a_skip_no_money=0; d07b_skip_no_money=0; d07a_skip_labels=5=Sought treatment at gvt health fac.; 6=Sought treatment at church/mission facility; 9=Went to local grocery for medicine.
remaining_caveat: Excluded leakage rows are not used to repair access outcomes; their presence remains a survey-quality caveat.

accepted_rule: Use government/private facilities as core formal care; add church/mission as an extended sensitivity.
remaining_caveat: Provider grouping is context/sensitivity here and does not change the cost-barrier forgone-care numerator.
